steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Transport Domain Separation
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 9222 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Change one Feed aggregation and Go format, URL, response, XML, constraint, migration, schedule, lease, goroutine, tenant, reader, deployment, or restore condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state controlled native transfer limits for the debug Feed Go Loss Report case

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-loss-report-1fsxuk" that uses case 3585 to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Go Loss Report into observable performance evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report, keep Feed Go Loss Report connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Loss Report precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 2464 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Emit field-level admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicted, and unsupported evidence for each normalization run.

For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Loss Report, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A normalized row need not explain information loss.
